changes_made: Changed k8s_resource_limits_configured to k8s_pod_resource_limits_configured for specificity
Changed k8s_horizontal_pod_autoscaler_enabled to k8s_pod_horizontal_autoscaler_enabled for consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to capacity management through resource limits, autoscaling, and monitoring. Naming conventions needed adjustments for specificity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology.
naming_improvements: Ensure the resource type is specified, such as 'pod' for resource limits and autoscaling.
title: Capacity Management
step1_reasoning: Capacity management in Kubernetes can be mapped to ensuring that resource limits and requests are set for workloads, enabling autoscaling features, and monitoring resource utilization at both the cluster and node levels. These practices help manage the capacity and performance of a Kubernetes environment, similar to how cloud monitoring and alarms are used to manage capacity in cloud environments.
